content:
Practical guidance for “Spare parts harvest log” within “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”. It supports clear responsibilities, verified information, visible records, fair priorities, stop conditions, and a workable handover, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Spare parts harvest log” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Stop condition: Do not taste food to decide whether contamination, spoilage, or flood exposure made it unsafe. Apply this boundary to “Spare parts harvest log” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Reduced fallback: If “Spare parts harvest log” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a smaller documented task with one owner, one deadline, and one independent check.

steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Spare parts harvest log” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Check packaging, date, storage history, allergens, pests, water exposure, and temperature control before use. Record the result under “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience” before continuing “Spare parts harvest log”.
Separate raw, cooked, ready-to-eat, allergen-sensitive, and questionable items. Recheck this point before closing “Spare parts harvest log” for “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Use the safest available water, clean hands and tools, adequate cooking, and protected serving. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Spare parts harvest log” for “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.
Label portions with contents, preparation time, priority users, and discard or review time. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Spare parts harvest log”.

Record consumption and losses so the next meal plan protects nutrition without hiding unsafe food. Include the outcome of “Spare parts harvest log” in the handover for “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.
warnings: Do not taste food to decide whether contamination, spoilage, or flood exposure made it unsafe. Apply this boundary to “Spare parts harvest log” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.
Keep fuel, pesticides, cleaning chemicals, waste, animals, and dirty water away from food areas. Apply this boundary to “Spare parts harvest log” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Stop and reassess “Spare parts harvest log” when users develop vomiting, diarrhoea, severe abdominal symptoms, allergic reaction, or unusual clustering of illness. Apply this boundary to “Spare parts harvest log” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.
